tasks.title: Snapshot baseline + confirm tests green (pre-upgrade gate)
tasks.description:
Establish a clear, reproducible pre-upgrade baseline before changing constraints.

In scope:
- Capture a dependency snapshot using `scripts/deps_audit.py`.
- Run the current offline test suite and record results.

Out of scope:
- Any dependency upgrades.
tasks.rationale: Makes later breakage triage faster by ensuring we know exactly what was working before upgrades begin.
implementation_notes: Generate a timestamped snapshot file (e.g. `/tmp/forge-deps-audit.md`).
Run `poetry run pytest -q` and keep output in the PR description for the first upgrade PR.
acceptance_criteria: `poetry run python scripts/deps_audit.py --output /tmp/forge-deps-audit.md` succeeds.
`poetry run pytest -q` passes on the current lockfile.
The dependency snapshot and test output are attached to the first upgrade PR description.

tasks.title: Reconcile pyproject constraints with lockfile (truthful constraints)
tasks.description:
Ensure `pyproject.toml` accurately reflects the repo’s intended dependency baselines and does not contradict `poetry.lock`.

In scope:
- Identify mismatches between declared constraints and resolved versions.
- Update constraints (or re-lock) so `poetry install` is reproducible and predictable.

Out of scope:
- Major-version upgrades (those happen in later tasks).
tasks.rationale: We cannot safely upgrade in phases if our declared constraints are already out of sync with what contributors are installing.
implementation_notes: Prefer tightening or widening bounds intentionally rather than leaving accidental mismatches.
Keep changes minimal and focused on consistency.
Run `poetry install` and `poetry run pytest -q` after reconciling.
acceptance_criteria: `poetry install` completes without prompting for unexpected resolver changes.
`poetry show --top-level --only main` aligns with the intended baseline constraints in `pyproject.toml`.
`poetry run pytest -q` still passes.

tasks.title: Optional: add CI gate for offline smoke tests
tasks.description:
Add a lightweight CI workflow that runs the offline test suite on supported Python versions.

In scope:
- A GitHub Actions workflow that runs `poetry install` and `poetry run pytest -q`.
- A small matrix (at least Python 3.11 and 3.12 once supported).

Out of scope:
- Online provider integration tests requiring API keys.
tasks.rationale: Upgrades become much safer when we have an automated green/red signal for the offline orchestration suite.
implementation_notes: Keep caching minimal and deterministic (Poetry cache + virtualenv).
Fail fast on install or test failures.
acceptance_criteria: A CI workflow exists and runs on PRs.
CI executes `poetry run pytest -q` successfully on at least one Python version.
CI is documented briefly in `docs/testing_guide.md` (command parity).

tasks.title: Upgrade dev toolchain (pytest/ruff/black/isort/mypy) without runtime changes
tasks.description:
Upgrade dev-only tools first to de-risk later runtime upgrades.

In scope:
- Update dev dependencies to modern versions (pytest, pytest-asyncio, ruff, black, isort, mypy, pytest-cov).
- Update tool configuration in `pyproject.toml` as required (especially Ruff config schema).

Out of scope:
- Runtime dependency upgrades (LangGraph/LangChain/provider SDKs).
tasks.rationale: Separating tooling churn from runtime churn keeps PR review and debugging manageable.
implementation_notes: Do the Ruff config migration in the same PR as the ruff bump to avoid split-brain configs.
Run formatters and keep diffs mechanical.
acceptance_criteria: `poetry run ruff check .` passes.
`poetry run black . --check` passes.
`poetry run isort . --check-only` passes.
`poetry run mypy anvil` passes.
`poetry run pytest -q` passes.

tasks.title: Bump Python support baseline to >=3.11,<3.13 and update tool target versions
tasks.description:
Update Forge’s supported Python versions to a modern baseline compatible with current LangGraph/LangChain ecosystems.

In scope:
- Update the Python constraint in `pyproject.toml`.
- Update Black/Ruff/Mypy target versions accordingly.
- Update docs to reflect supported Python versions.

Out of scope:
- Any LangGraph/LangChain upgrades (those are separate tasks).
tasks.rationale: Modern orchestration libraries and integrations increasingly assume newer Python; aligning early reduces upgrade friction later.
implementation_notes: Ensure local developer commands in docs remain accurate for the new baseline.
acceptance_criteria: `pyproject.toml` declares `python >=3.11,<3.13` (or a clearly justified alternative).
Black/Ruff/Mypy target versions are updated and consistent with the Python baseline.
`poetry run pytest -q` passes on the new baseline (at least locally on 3.11).
`docs/installation.md` reflects the new Python requirement.

tasks.title: Upgrade core runtime deps (pydantic/python-dotenv/pyyaml/tenacity/orjson) and validate
tasks.description:
Upgrade non-orchestration core runtime dependencies to the newest compatible versions.

In scope:
- Upgrade: `pydantic`, `python-dotenv`, `pyyaml`, `tenacity`, `orjson` (and any required transitive bumps).
- Fix any runtime breakage due to minor API changes.

Out of scope:
- LangGraph/LangChain/provider SDK upgrades (handled separately).
tasks.rationale: Keeping the foundational runtime stack current reduces security risk and avoids compounding upgrade work later.
implementation_notes: Keep upgrades focused; avoid wide resolver churn.
Run the full offline test suite after upgrading.
acceptance_criteria: `poetry update pydantic python-dotenv pyyaml tenacity orjson` completes successfully.
`poetry run pytest -q` passes.
No new mandatory dependencies are introduced for default installs.

tasks.title: Upgrade optional CLI/API deps (rich/typer/fastapi/uvicorn) and validate install remains optional
tasks.description:
Upgrade optional extras that should not affect default contributors.

In scope:
- Upgrade: `rich`, `typer`, `fastapi`, `uvicorn`.
- Ensure the repo does not import these at runtime unless the extras are installed.

Out of scope:
- Implementing an API server (not currently present in the repo).
tasks.rationale: These packages are outdated and have security/compat improvements, but should remain optional to keep installs lightweight.
implementation_notes: Verify imports remain guarded where appropriate.
Update docs that mention these extras if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Default `poetry install` still succeeds without installing `api`/`cli` extras.
`poetry run pytest -q` passes without extras installed.
`docs/installation.md` accurately describes how to install `api` and `cli` extras.

tasks.title: Upgrade LangGraph + checkpoint packages to 1.x/3.x baseline
tasks.description:
Upgrade Forge’s orchestration engine to modern LangGraph baselines.

In scope:
- Upgrade `langgraph` to the 1.x line.
- Upgrade `langgraph-checkpoint` to the 3.x line.
- Fix compilation/runtime breakage due to LangGraph API changes.

Out of scope:
- Changing the pipeline semantics (nodes/edges/behavior) beyond what’s required to keep current behavior working.
tasks.rationale: LangGraph 1.x unlocks the current orchestration API surface needed for more advanced agent pipelines.
implementation_notes: Keep changes isolated to LangGraph integration points (`langgraph_builder.py`, `langgraph_executor.py`).
Expect checkpointing import paths to change.
acceptance_criteria: `LangGraphExecutor` can compile and run the graph in memory checkpoint mode.
`poetry run pytest -q` passes (offline suite is the gate).
No new runtime dependency on a separate LangGraph service is introduced.

tasks.title: Restore SQLite checkpointing under LangGraph 1.x (add sqlite saver package + lifecycle)
tasks.description:
Make `FORGE_LG_CHECKPOINT=sqlite` work on LangGraph 1.x.

In scope:
- Add `langgraph-checkpoint-sqlite` dependency.
- Refactor `LangGraphExecutor`/builder so the SQLite saver is acquired/closed correctly.

Out of scope:
- Changing the default checkpointing mode (should remain memory by default unless configured).
tasks.rationale: LangGraph’s SQLite saver is split into a separate distribution in newer versions; Forge must explicitly support it to keep documented functionality working.
implementation_notes: Decide on saver lifetime: per-run (simpler for CLI) vs long-lived executor-managed context.
Ensure sqlite path configuration remains `FORGE_LG_DB_PATH` compatible.
acceptance_criteria: With sqlite checkpoint mode enabled, the graph compiles and executes without resource leaks.
SQLite checkpointer creation works even when invoked multiple times in one process.
`poetry run pytest -q` passes.

tasks.title: Harden streaming event normalization for LangGraph 1.x
tasks.description:
Ensure `LangGraphExecutor.stream_execution()` continues to produce stable normalized events after the LangGraph major upgrade.

In scope:
- Make event parsing tolerant to upstream schema drift.
- Keep the normalized event dict keys stable (`event`, `node`, `time`, `data`, optional `final_state`).

Out of scope:
- Reworking CLI streaming UX.
tasks.rationale: Streaming is a primary observability path for advanced pipelines; upgrades should not silently degrade the stream contract.
implementation_notes: Avoid hard assumptions about where `output.state` lives; keep best-effort extraction.
Don’t fail the stream on missing keys.
acceptance_criteria: `tests/test_lg_offline_smoke.py::test_offline_streaming` passes.
Streaming still emits at least one event per node execution in the offline smoke test.
No KeyError/TypeError crashes occur during streaming under the upgraded LangGraph.

tasks.title: Add sqlite-mode offline smoke coverage
tasks.description:
Extend the offline smoke tests to exercise SQLite checkpointing end-to-end.

In scope:
- Add a test that runs `LangGraphExecutor(checkpoint="sqlite")` using a temp db path.
- Verify it completes and returns a final `ForgeState`.

Out of scope:
- Testing resume/replay semantics (that can be a future enhancement once the baseline is stable).
tasks.rationale: SQLite saver lifecycle changes are easy to get subtly wrong; tests prevent regressions.
implementation_notes: Use pytest temp directory fixtures for db files.
Avoid relying on external state; keep tests hermetic.
acceptance_criteria: A new offline test covers sqlite checkpoint mode without network calls.
The sqlite test passes on repeated runs (no leftover lockfiles or handles).
`poetry run pytest -q` passes.

tasks.title: Update checkpointing docs for modern LangGraph saver packaging
tasks.description:
Update documentation to match the upgraded checkpointing implementation.

In scope:
- Update `docs/checkpointing.md` to reflect sqlite saver packaging/behavior.
- Ensure environment variable examples remain correct.

Out of scope:
- Adding new persistence backends beyond sqlite/memory.
tasks.rationale: If checkpointing behavior changes, docs must remain copy/paste accurate to avoid confusing users.
implementation_notes: Call out that sqlite saver may be provided by a separate distribution depending on the LangGraph baseline.
acceptance_criteria: `docs/checkpointing.md` matches the current code behavior for memory vs sqlite modes.
Docs include the correct install guidance for enabling sqlite checkpointing (if it requires an extra package).

tasks.title: Upgrade LangChain core + integrations to 1.x baseline
tasks.description:
Upgrade Forge’s provider adapter layer to the modern LangChain ecosystem.

In scope:
- Upgrade `langchain-core`, `langchain`, `langchain-openai`, `langchain-anthropic` to current stable baselines.
- Fix provider adapter breakage caused by API shifts.

Out of scope:
- Refactoring orchestration nodes to new LangChain patterns beyond what providers need to keep current behavior working.
tasks.rationale: Modern LangChain integrations provide better tooling and structured-output capabilities that future advanced LangGraph pipelines will depend on.
implementation_notes: Prefer updating provider init kwargs to `api_key=` but keep compatibility where reasonable.
Keep changes localized to provider modules and shared provider base classes.
acceptance_criteria: `poetry run pytest -q` passes (offline gate).
Provider modules import successfully when their extras are installed.
No changes are required to default workflows that do not install provider extras.

tasks.title: Modernize OpenAIProvider initialization + kwargs mapping for LangChain 1.x
tasks.description:
Update `anvil/providers/openai.py` to match modern LangChain OpenAI integration expectations.

In scope:
- Prefer `api_key=` (or equivalent modern fields) over legacy `openai_api_key=` where supported.
- Ensure role-based kwargs still bind correctly.

Out of scope:
- Adding new OpenAI features (tools, responses API) unless required for compatibility.
tasks.rationale: Constructor kwargs and param names can deprecate quickly; updating proactively avoids future breakage.
implementation_notes: Keep the provider API stable (`generate`, `chat`, `embed`).
Normalize token usage extraction if metadata location changes.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAIProvider can be instantiated when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set and openai extras installed.
Role-based overrides still flow through `.bind(**kwargs)` as expected.
`poetry run pytest -q` passes.

tasks.title: Modernize AnthropicProvider initialization + kwargs mapping for LangChain 1.x
tasks.description:
Update `anvil/providers/anthropic.py` to match modern LangChain Anthropic integration expectations.

In scope:
- Prefer modern constructor fields (`api_key=`) where supported.
- Ensure `_convert_anthropic_params` produces compatible kwargs for the chosen call path.
- Keep low-level SDK fallback behavior consistent with test expectations.

Out of scope:
- Implementing new Anthropic features (thinking, betas) unless required for compatibility.
tasks.rationale: Anthropic integration APIs shift across versions; keeping a stable adapter is critical for orchestration nodes that rely on it.
implementation_notes: Keep tests mock-based and offline.
Validate both the low-level SDK path and the LangChain wrapper fallback remain coherent.
acceptance_criteria: `tests/providers/test_anthropic.py` passes.
AnthropicProvider works when `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is set and anthropic extras installed.
`poetry run pytest -q` passes.

tasks.title: Audit and update LangChainProvider base wrapper for LangChain 1.x response/kwargs changes
tasks.description:
Ensure `anvil/providers/base.py` remains compatible with LangChain 1.x invocation and message/metadata shapes.

In scope:
- Validate `.invoke(...)` call patterns still work.
- Verify `_convert_kwargs` does not pass invalid parameters to new wrappers.
- Ensure the base class doesn’t silently break provider-specific overrides.

Out of scope:
- Rewriting the provider layer architecture (keep changes minimal).
tasks.rationale: Most providers depend on shared base behavior; a subtle change here can break the whole pipeline.
implementation_notes: Prefer provider-specific normalization in provider modules when possible.
Keep `extract_token_usage` best-effort and non-fatal.
acceptance_criteria: `poetry run pytest -q` passes.
No new runtime warnings/errors are emitted during offline smoke tests due to invalid kwargs.
Provider adapters still accept the same public kwargs surface (temperature/max_tokens/etc.).

tasks.title: Update LangChain integration docs to modern Runnable-style examples
tasks.description:
Refresh `docs/langchain_integration.md` so that copy/paste examples align with the LangChain 1.x ecosystem.

In scope:
- Replace deprecated examples with runnable-based examples where appropriate.
- Ensure install instructions match the actual extras (`langchain-openai`, `langchain-anthropic`).

Out of scope:
- Adding new end-to-end tutorials beyond updating existing docs.
tasks.rationale: Docs that lag behind library versions become a support burden and slow down adoption.
implementation_notes: Prefer minimal examples that do not require network calls unless explicitly stated.
acceptance_criteria: `docs/langchain_integration.md` contains examples that import successfully with the upgraded dependencies installed.
Version guidance in the doc matches the declared constraints in `pyproject.toml`.

tasks.title: Update setup tooling to reflect upgraded package layout (import checks stay accurate)
tasks.description:
Ensure `scripts/setup_tools.py` and related setup helpers correctly detect installed extras after dependency upgrades.

In scope:
- Verify import checks match the actual integration packages.
- Update messaging/instructions if extras or import names changed.

Out of scope:
- Adding new setup flows or installers.
tasks.rationale: Contributor setup should remain smooth across upgrades, especially when extras split into separate packages.
implementation_notes: Run `poetry run python scripts/setup_tools.py --check-only` as a smoke check after upgrades.
acceptance_criteria: Setup tool correctly reports missing vs installed modules for each extra.
No false negatives for `langgraph` or provider extras after upgrades.

tasks.title: Upgrade OpenAI SDK to 2.x and verify provider behavior remains compatible
tasks.description:
Upgrade the OpenAI Python SDK to the newest compatible major and ensure Forge remains stable.

In scope:
- Upgrade `openai` to 2.x.
- Ensure `langchain-openai` remains compatible with the selected OpenAI SDK version.

Out of scope:
- Adding OpenAI Responses API usage in Forge (unless required by the integration wrapper).
tasks.rationale: Staying current with provider SDKs improves reliability/security and enables newer model capabilities as we expand pipelines.
implementation_notes: Focus on import-time compatibility and LangChain wrapper expectations first.
Avoid introducing direct SDK calls unless needed.
acceptance_criteria: `poetry run pytest -q` passes.
OpenAIProvider still initializes and can invoke the LangChain wrapper (mocking is acceptable in tests).
No dependency conflicts between `openai` and `langchain-openai`.

tasks.title: Upgrade Anthropic SDK to 0.75 and keep mock-based provider tests stable
tasks.description:
Upgrade the Anthropic Python SDK to the newest compatible version and ensure Forge’s low-level client path remains correct.

In scope:
- Upgrade `anthropic` to ~0.75.
- Update `anvil/providers/anthropic.py` and `tests/providers/test_anthropic.py` if patch points or response shapes changed.

Out of scope:
- Implementing new Anthropic SDK features beyond compatibility fixes.
tasks.rationale: Forge uses the low-level Anthropic client for testability and sometimes direct calls; SDK upgrades are likely to change patch points or response shapes.
implementation_notes: Keep tests patching the stable constructor path (update patch target only if required by SDK changes).
Maintain best-effort usage extraction via `anvil/usage.py`.
acceptance_criteria: `tests/providers/test_anthropic.py` passes.
`poetry run pytest -q` passes.
AnthropicProvider still supports both low-level client mode and LangChain fallback mode.

tasks.title: Upgrade optional Transformers stack (transformers/torch/accelerate/hf-hub/tokenizers) behind extras
tasks.description:
Upgrade optional ML stack packages while keeping them opt-in.

In scope:
- Upgrade: `transformers`, `torch`, `accelerate`, `huggingface-hub`, `tokenizers`.
- Keep these behind the `transformers` extra.
- Document platform-specific notes if constraints become OS/arch specific.

Out of scope:
- Guaranteeing GPU builds on every platform (keep scope to dependency correctness and importability).
tasks.rationale: Advanced pipelines often require local model experimentation; keeping the optional stack current improves capability while preserving lightweight defaults.
implementation_notes: Prefer minimal constraints that work across macOS/Linux where feasible.
Ensure default `poetry install` remains fast and does not pull heavy dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: `poetry install --extras transformers` succeeds on the primary dev platform.
`poetry run pytest -q` passes without requiring the transformers extra.
`docs/installation.md` includes any necessary platform notes for optional ML installs.

tasks.title: Upgrade optional RL stack (skrl + dependencies) behind extras
tasks.description:
Upgrade optional RL components (SKRL and its ecosystem) while keeping them opt-in.

In scope:
- Upgrade `skrl` and key dependencies (e.g., `gymnasium`) as needed.
- Ensure `poetry install --extras rl` works.

Out of scope:
- Adding RL training features to Forge (this is dependency modernization only).
tasks.rationale: If RL remains part of the leadership roadmap, the dependency stack must remain installable and reasonably current.
implementation_notes: Keep changes limited to dependency constraints unless something breaks import-time.
acceptance_criteria: `poetry install --extras rl` succeeds on the primary dev platform.
`poetry run pytest -q` passes without requiring the rl extra.
Docs remain clear that RL is optional.

tasks.title: Docs sweep: reflect new dependency baselines and upgrade commands
tasks.description:
Update docs to match the upgraded dependency baselines once the upgrade program completes.

In scope:
- Ensure `README.md`, `docs/installation.md`, `docs/testing_guide.md`, and `docs/provider_support.md` reflect the final supported versions and install commands.
- Ensure any version guidance is consistent with `pyproject.toml`.

Out of scope:
- Writing new architecture docs; this is about keeping existing docs accurate.
tasks.rationale: After a large upgrade, documentation drift is the fastest way to re-introduce confusion and setup failures.
implementation_notes: Prefer referencing `poetry install --extras ...` commands that match current extras definitions.
Remove mentions of deprecated versions/APIs where possible.
acceptance_criteria: Docs mention the correct Python baseline and the correct extras install commands.
Docs do not refer to removed/renamed LangGraph checkpoint saver locations.
All docs changes are validated by a quick `rg` search for old version lines where applicable.

tasks.title: Finalize upgrade: record known-good version set and mark ADR-0002 accepted
tasks.description:
Once phases are complete, finalize the program by recording the known-good versions and updating ADR status.

In scope:
- Update `docs/adr/ADR-0002-dependency-upgrade-and-langgraph-modernization.md` status from draft to accepted.
- Add a short appendix with the final selected baselines (LangGraph/LangChain/provider SDKs) and any pins/upper bounds we intentionally set.

Out of scope:
- Publishing a release or tagging a version (do separately if desired).
tasks.rationale: Without a recorded known-good set, we lose the benefit of the upgrade program’s discipline and repeatability.
implementation_notes: Use `poetry show --top-level --only main` as the source of truth for the final baseline list.
acceptance_criteria: `docs/adr/ADR-0002-dependency-upgrade-and-langgraph-modernization.md` status is updated to accepted.
ADR includes the final chosen baseline versions and any intentional pins.
`poetry run pytest -q` passes on the final lockfile.
